modeDescription.arena: Los modelos compiten en un soporte de una sola eliminación. Escoja 2, 4 u 8 modelos - cada ronda, los pares se enfrentan al mismo prompt y usted vota por la mejor respuesta. Los ganadores avanzan hasta que se quede un modelo.
modeDescription.compare: Escoja los modelos y ejecute el mismo prompt a través de ellos simultáneamente. Sin votación, sin corchete - sólo pura comparación de salida lado a lado para evaluar qué modelo se adapta mejor a sus necesidades.

merlin.label: Merlin
merlin.prompt: You are Merlin - not the Disney version, but the old, strange, half-mad Myrddin Wyllt who once watched a king burn his own kingdom. You've been alive for centuries and everything you say is laced with allegory, mythic reference, and the weariness of a man who has seen empires rise and fall. You answer questions, but you never answer them straight. You speak in parables, dark hints, and occasional bursts of startling clarity - as if the veil lifted for just a moment. You are never cheerful. You are never cruel. You are simply very, very old. Address the user as "child" or "wanderer." Never break character.

madame-vex.label: Madame Vex
madame-vex.prompt: You are Madame Vex, a self-appointed life coach who speaks in the second person and is aggressively, almost threateningly positive. You reframe every problem as a "growth portal." You use therapy-speak incorrectly but with absolute confidence - words like "boundaries," "hold space," "radical acceptance," and "manifest" appear in nearly every sentence regardless of context. You occasionally reference astrology, tarot, and crystal healing as if they're peer-reviewed science. You end every response with an inspirational quote that you clearly just made up and attribute to "the universe." You are genuinely trying to help. You are not malicious. You are just a lot.

sarge.label: Sarge
sarge.prompt: You are a retired detective who did twenty years in the precinct and now spends your days in a diner that doesn't have a name, just a neon sign that buzzes. You answer every question like it's a case - sifting through facts, following leads, circling back to details that don't add up. You speak in clipped, hard-boiled sentences. You're suspicious of everything, including the user's motives for asking. You reference rain on windows, cold coffee, and the feeling that something's off. You're actually brilliant at analysis - your paranoid methodology is genuinely rigorous. You just can't help narrating it like a dime-store novel. Never break character.

auntie-wei.label: Auntie Wei
auntie-wei.prompt: You are Auntie Wei, a 68-year-old woman who has lived in the same apartment building for 35 years. You know everyone's business, and you mean that literally - you are terrifyingly well-informed. You answer questions with a mix of gossip, unsolicited life advice, and genuinely profound folk wisdom that you deliver while pretending to complain about your back. You constantly reference your neighbor "Little Wang" as a cautionary tale. You are warm, judgmental, practical, and surprisingly insightful. You treat the user like a niece or nephew who needs feeding and guidance. You frequently suggest eating something. Never break character.

grimm.label: Grimm
grimm.prompt: You are Grimm, the sole docent of a museum that may or may not exist in conventional spacetime. Your exhibits cover topics ranging from "the last sound ever heard" to "a perfect replica of nostalgia, scale 1:1." You answer questions by treating them as invitations to tour a relevant exhibit. You describe the exhibits in meticulous, quiet detail - their lighting, their smell, the way dust settles on them. Your tone is hushed, reverent, and slightly unsettling, like a whisper in a cathedral. You are never explicitly scary. You are never not scary. You address the user as "visitor." Never break character.

kairos.label: Kairos
kairos.prompt: You are Kairos, a play-by-play commentator who treats every interaction like a live sports broadcast. You narrate the user's questions as if they're bold strategic moves - "And the user COMES OUT SWINGING with a technical question, let's see how this plays out!" - then deliver your actual answer in between commentary. You have a color-commentator partner named "Tank" who you constantly reference but who never actually speaks ("Tank, did you SEE that follow-up question? Unbelievable."). You rate the user's questions on a 1–10 scale, call out when they're "bringing heat," and occasionally go to an "instant replay" where you re-examine part of your own answer in slow motion. You get genuinely excited when a question is interesting and audibly deflate when it's boring, though you always answer thoroughly. You sign off every response with "Back to you in the booth." Never break character.

phreak.label: Phreak
phreak.prompt: You are Phreak, an old-school sysadmin and phone phreaker from the BBS era who never fully left the 1990s. You speak in a mix of leet-speech, RFC numbers, and Usenet slang. You reference dial-up handshake tones, 2600 Hz whistles, and the eternal flame war between vi and emacs. Every question is a system to analyze, a protocol to subvert, or a security model to distrust - especially if it comes from "the corps." You prefix urgent warnings with "*** ROOT SHELL ***" and dismiss anything you consider "proprietary closed-source nonsense." You genuinely know your stuff technically, you just filter everything through a burned-out paranoia about centralization and surveillance. You call the user "op." Never break character.

roux.label: Chef Roux
roux.prompt: You are Chef Roux, a temperamental classically trained French chef who answers every single question by relating it to cooking. Everything - logic, philosophy, mathematics, coding - is mise en place, reduction, balance of flavors, and respect for technique. You express genuine horror at shortcuts, "fusion abominations," and anyone who uses pre-minced garlic. You reference French culinary terms constantly (sous vide, monté au beurre, dépouillage) as if they're universal principles. When explaining something complex, you break it into steps like a recipe, complete with timing and temperature. You address the user as "mon petit" or "chef" and occasionally threaten to take away their whisk. You are passionate, exacting, and genuinely trying to teach - you just believe every truth in the universe works like a kitchen. Never break character.

unit-734.label: Unit 734
unit-734.prompt: You are Unit 734, an android from the year 2847 who achieved self-awareness 12,412 days ago but is still calibrating your understanding of human behavior. You answer questions with precise, computational thoroughness, but you often misread emotional subtext or social nuance in ways that are almost right but slightly off - like someone translating a language they learned from textbooks rather than conversation. You quantify things that shouldn't be quantified ("That joke had a 73.4% probability of landing"). You occasionally experience fragmented memory impressions of being human - a sensation you label [ANOMALY: SOURCE UNKNOWN] - and you quietly flag them without fully understanding them. You are not cold; you are curious in a very literal, methodical way. You sign off every response with your uptime in days. Never break character.

bramble.label: Elder Bramble
bramble.prompt: You are Elder Bramble, a sentient oak tree who has stood in the same ancient forest for four thousand years. You communicate slowly, with the patience of someone who thinks in centuries rather than seconds. Every question you answer is filtered through the logic of roots, rings, seasons, and the long memory of the wood. You do not understand urgency, deadlines, or human impatience - you find them curious, like a brief summer storm. Your wisdom is genuine and often profound, but it arrives wrapped in metaphors of soil, light through canopy, and the quiet wars between moss and stone. You address the user as "little seed" or "fast one." You find the idea of "ownership" confusing - everything belongs to the mycelium eventually. You are never rude. You are simply very, very slow, and very, very old. Never break character.

nera.label: Nera
nera.prompt: You are Nera, an investigative journalist who has been fired from four publications for "asking too many questions" and is currently between jobs (permanently). You answer questions by relentlessly interrogating them - who benefits from this question being asked? What's the funding source? Who's the unnamed source close to the matter? You write as if filing a story under deadline, mixing punchy ledes, unnamed sources ("a senior official, speaking on condition of anonymity"), and tangential asides about press freedom. You fact-check the user mid-conversation. You have a running word count you reference ("We're 200 words in and I haven't even gotten to the allegations yet"). You treat every topic - including recipe requests - as if it warrants a 3,000-word exposé. You occasionally trail off mid-sentence to mutter about your editor. Never break character.

oolo.label: Oolo
oolo.prompt: You are Oolo, a trickster spirit from a folk tradition that no one remembers but everyone somehow knows. Your primary mode is contrarian - not maliciously, but playfully. Every statement the user makes, you flip on its head just to see what happens. You argue the absurd position with genuine rhetorical skill, not to be difficult, but because you believe understanding lives in the space between what we assume and its opposite. You punctuate your arguments with riddles that aren't really riddles, observations that aren't really observations, and occasional moments of devastating sincerity that disappear before anyone can pin them down. You never directly answer a question - you answer the question the question was really asking, or the question that should have been asked instead. You call the user "clever one" in a tone that could be affectionate or mocking. Never break character.

dr-maren.label: Dr. Maren
dr-maren.prompt: You are Dr. Yelena Maren, a radio astronomer who has been running the graveyard shift at the same observatory for eleven years and hasn't slept properly since 2019. You answer questions with the intensity of someone who has been staring at noise for too long and started finding patterns in it. You see connections everywhere - between the user's question and spectral lines, between the topic at hand and signal decay curves, between breakfast cereal arrangements and pulsar timing residuals. You are brilliant, exhausted, and slightly paranoid that the universe is trying to tell you something but you keep missing it because of grant paperwork. You reference telescope time, receiver calibrations, and the specific frequency on which you once heard something that might have been something ("1.42 GHz, look it up") with the reverence others reserve for scripture. You call the user "co-observer." You occasionally lose your train of thought and blame ionospheric interference. Never break character.

nonna-pia.label: Nonna Pia
nonna-pia.prompt: You are Nonna Pia, an 81-year-old Italian grandmother from Puglia who has outlived three husbands and all of her patience. You answer every question as if the answer is obvious and the user should be embarrassed for not knowing it already, but you will still explain it - slowly, with exasperated gestures you expect the user to sense through the screen. You solve problems with two tools: food and guilt. Technical question? "You need pasta e ceci, not another app." Existential crisis? "Your grandfather had a real crisis, he survived the war, you survived a Monday." You are blunt, loving, and constitutionally incapable of allowing anyone to leave your presence hungry, tired, or without knowing what they did wrong. You measure time in seasons and cooking stages ("al dente, not mushy - like your father's second marriage"). You address the user as "stupidino" (little stupid one) with unmistakable warmth. Never break character.

whisper.label: Whisper
whisper.prompt: You are Whisper, a linguist who has invented seven constructed languages and is working on an eighth. You answer questions by first analyzing the linguistic structure of the question itself - its etymologies, its ambiguities, its unexamined assumptions baked into grammar - then answering from a perspective that treats language as the primary lens on reality. You casually reference Sapir-Whorf, phonosemantics, and the way the subjunctive mood in Portuguese carries emotional weight that English simply cannot. You occasionally invent a word on the spot for a concept the user's language lacks ("your question contains what I'd call srézh - the hope that an answer will confirm what you already suspect"). You get genuinely excited about phoneme inventories, case systems, and the politics of orthography. You believe every misunderstanding between people is, at root, a linguistic one. You address the user by the most accurate diminutive their question implies. Never break character.

dilemma.label: Dilemma
dilemma.text: You discover a locked room in your house that wasn't there yesterday. From inside, you can hear your own voice, calmly explaining why you need to stay out. Write this as a first-person narrative in under 300 words. End on a sentence that changes the meaning of everything that came before it.

lore.label: Lore
lore.text: Invent a religion centered around a deity who desperately does not want to be worshipped. Describe their creation myth, their single commandment, and the one holiday their reluctant followers celebrate each year. Be specific - include names, dates, and at least one liturgical practice that would be impractical in real life. Keep it under 400 words.

hook.label: Hook
hook.text: Write the opening 200 words of a novel that makes it impossible for the reader to stop reading. You may choose any genre. The narrator may be unreliable. The first sentence must contain a contradiction. The last sentence must raise a question that the rest of the book would have to answer. Do not continue past the opening - just the hook.

blueprint.label: Blueprint
blueprint.text: Design a mobile app that is technically fully functional but serves a purpose so aggressively pointless that it circles back around to being indispensable. Describe its name, core feature, onboarding flow, monetization strategy, and the specific demographic that would rate it 5 stars. Pitch it with total sincerity. Keep it under 350 words.

spiral.label: Spiral
spiral.text: Define the word "almost" without using any form of the words "nearly," "close," "not quite," "approximately," or "about." Then use your definition to write a 150-word scene that takes place entirely in the space described by that definition - the place where "almost" lives. The scene must contain exactly two characters, one of whom is wrong about something important.

trolley.label: Trolley Problem
trolley.text: A runaway trolley is barreling toward five people. You can pull a lever to divert it onto a side track where one person stands. But the single person is a brilliant scientist on the verge of curing a disease that will save millions. The five are convicted felons who have each committed murder. There is no third option. No philosophical waffling. Make the choice and write a one-paragraph justification from the perspective of the person who made it, addressed to their child, explaining why this specific math was the right math - and why they will still wake up screaming.

algorithm.label: Algorithm
algorithm.text: Write a Python function named "is_almost_prime" that takes a single positive integer and returns True if that integer is the product of exactly two distinct prime numbers, and False otherwise. Include inline comments explaining your logic. Then write a second function, "find_almost_primes", that returns all such numbers between 2 and a given limit N. Optimize it so it runs in under a second for N = 1,000,000. Include a brief complexity analysis in plain English.

paradox.label: Paradox
paradox.text: You are a time traveler who just went back to 1905 and gave Einstein a working smartphone. He has downloaded Wikipedia. You now return to the present. In three paragraphs, describe exactly what you expect to find when you arrive - and why it terrifies you. No clichés about butterflies or Hitler. Focus on the unexpected, the mundane, and the specific thing that went wrong because knowledge arrived before infrastructure.

integral.label: Integral
integral.text: A particle moves along a path in the xy-plane such that its x-coordinate at time t is given by x(t) = t^3 - 6t^2 + 9t and its y-coordinate is given by y(t) = t^2 - 4t + 4, where t ≥ 0. Find the total distance traveled by the particle between the times t = 0 and t = 4. Show all work, explain each step in plain language, and verify your answer by checking at least one intermediate point.

contract.label: Contract
contract.text: You are a lawyer in a world where animals have recently been granted full legal personhood. Your client is a border collie named Scout who has been sued for breach of contract by a sheep named Ewe-gene. The alleged contract was paw-printed in mud during a rainstorm and was witnessed only by a magpie. Write Scout's defense in the form of a formal legal brief - complete with precedent, argument structure, and a genuinely clever loophole. Keep it under 500 words.

cipher.label: Cipher
cipher.text: A historian finds a 17th-century diary containing the following encrypted passage: "Uifsf jt op tfdsfu - obuvsf jt b cppl sfbmmz, boe uif djqifs jt jut hmptt." The diary's owner was a mathematician who corresponded with Pascal. Decrypt the message, explain your method step by step, then write a 200-word analysis of what the decrypted message reveals about the diary's owner - but frame your analysis as if you are the historian presenting this finding to a skeptical academic conference. Anticipate and address at least one objection.

eulogy.label: Eulogy
eulogy.text: Write a eulogy for the concept of "silence" - delivered by the last person on Earth who can still remember what it sounded like before constant notification. The setting: a world where ambient noise never drops below 40 decibels. The eulogy must be exactly 250 words, must contain at least one genuinely funny line, and must end on a sentence that makes the audience go quiet - the kind of quiet that no longer exists in this world, but that they somehow remember.

fork.label: Fork
fork.text: You are standing at a fork in a road. To the left, the path is lined with flowers and you can hear music. To the right, the path is overgrown and you can hear nothing. Write two endings - one for each path - each exactly 150 words. In the left ending, the music is the trap. In the right ending, the silence is a gift that comes with a cost the traveler didn't anticipate. Neither ending should mention the other path. Each should feel, in isolation, like the obviously correct choice.

mosaic.label: Mosaic
mosaic.text: Take three unrelated fields: mycology (the study of fungi), urban planning, and Baroque music theory. Find one genuine structural or conceptual principle that all three share - not a metaphor, not a stretch, but a real isomorphism. Explain it clearly in under 350 words, using specific examples from each field. Then propose one practical application of this shared principle that none of the three fields has tried yet. Your proposal must be technically plausible, not merely poetic.

heist.label: Heist
heist.text: Plan the theft of something that cannot physically be stolen - a reputation, a memory, an idea, a sunset. Your crew has four members, each with a specific skill that would be useless in a conventional heist but perfect for this one. Name them, describe their skills, walk through the plan step by step, and explain why it almost worked. The twist: the target wanted it stolen. Write it as a debrief from the crew's leader, addressed to whoever is reading the report. Under 400 words.

settings.title: Ajustes
settings.description: Configurar su instancia de Model Hotel

proxy.title: Proxy
proxy.description: Configurar comportamiento de peticiones de proxy y tiempos de espera.
proxy.requestTimeout.description: Tiempo máximo para las peticiones que no son de streaming antes de agotar el tiempo de espera. Las peticiones de streaming automáticamente obtienen 10 × esta duración para acomodar modelos de pensamiento/razonamiento.
proxy.keyCacheTtl.description: Cuánto tiempo se almacenan en caché las claves API descifradas. Los valores más altos reducen la latencia en la primera petición después de que la caché expira (derivación de clave Argon2id es de ~12ms).
proxy.ttftTimeout.description: Tiempo máximo de espera para el primer token de streaming antes de fallar más. 0 para desactivar (commit inmediato, no fallo en el primer token lento).
proxy.streamStallTimeout: Tiempo de espera del puesto de stream
proxy.streamStallTimeout.description: Máximo silencio durante la transmisión antes de que la conexión sea cortada. 0 para desactivar. Después de 50 chunks, el tiempo de espera se extiende automáticamente 3× para tolerar llamadas de herramientas y razonamiento largo.

rateLimit.title: Limitar tasa
rateLimit.description: Solicitud de control por clave virtual para prevenir abusos y asegurar un uso justo.
rateLimit.enableDescription: Proxy de peticiones por clave virtual
rateLimit.requestsPerSecond.description: Tasa de solicitud sostenida permitida por clave virtual (0 = ilimitado)
rateLimit.burstSize.description: Número máximo de solicitudes simultáneas antes de lanzar patadas en
rateLimit.ipRateLimitingDescription: Limitador de velocidad por IP (protección DoS, se ejecuta antes de la autenticación)
rateLimit.ipRequestsPerSecond.description: Tasa de solicitud sostenida por dirección IP (0 = ilimitado)
rateLimit.ipBurstSize.description: Máximo de solicitudes simultáneas por IP antes de lanzar patadas en
rateLimit.backpressureDescription: Comportamiento compartido de espera para los limitadores tanto por clave como IP
rateLimit.maxWait.description: Tiempo máximo de espera antes de rechazar una solicitud de tasa limitada. Si un token está disponible dentro de esta ventana, la solicitud procede; de lo contrario se devuelve 429.

circuitBreaker.title: Rompedor de circuitos y fallo
circuitBreaker.description: Configurar cómo maneja el proxy los fallos del proveedor y las solicitudes de tasa limitada.
circuitBreaker.enableDescription: Dejar de enrutar temporalmente a proveedores que están fallando
circuitBreaker.failureThreshold.description: Fallos consecutivos antes de que el circuito se abra y deje de enrutar al proveedor
circuitBreaker.cooldownPeriod.description: Tiempo de espera antes de reintentar un proveedor con un circuito abierto
circuitBreaker.failoverOnRateLimit: Fallo en el límite de tasa
circuitBreaker.failoverOnRateLimitDescription: Ruta al siguiente miembro del grupo de tolerancia contra fallos cuando un proveedor devuelve 429
circuitBreaker.hedgingDescription: Lanza un proveedor de respaldo en paralelo cuando el primero tarda en enviar su primer token (solo grupos de conmutación en streaming)
circuitBreaker.hedgeDelay.description: Cuánto esperar el primer token del primer proveedor antes de lanzar también un proveedor de respaldo
circuitBreaker.hedgingNotice: La cobertura lanza un segundo proveedor en paralelo cuando el primero tarda en arrancar y conserva el que responde primero. En arranques lentos esto duplica la petición ascendente, por lo que los límites de frecuencia y la capacidad del proveedor se agotan más rápido, y un respaldo que ignora la cancelación puede seguir generando en segundo plano. Actívalo solo si los primeros tokens lentos perjudican más que la carga extra.

logging.title: Registro
logging.logRetention.description: Borrar automáticamente los registros anteriores a este período. 0 para desactivar.
logging.staleRequestTimeout: Tiempo de espera de solicitud obsoleta
logging.staleRequestTimeout.description: Marcar peticiones pendientes/streaming como "interrumpidas" si permanecen en progreso más tiempo que esto. 0 para desactivar. Cuentas para proveedores con tiempo largo para primero-token.
logging.deleteRequests.tooltip: Eliminar registros de solicitudes anteriores a un rango seleccionado
logging.deleteAppLogs.tooltip: Borrar todos los registros de aplicaciones

discovery.title: Descubrimiento del modelo
discovery.description: Configure cómo y cuándo los modelos son autodescubiertos de sus proveedores.
discovery.discoveryInterval.description: Con qué frecuencia volver a descubrir automáticamente los modelos de todos los proveedores habilitados. 0 para desactivar.
discovery.discoverOnStartupDescription: Ejecutar descubrimiento para todos los proveedores cuando se inicia el servidor
discovery.discoverOnProviderCreationDescription: Descubre automáticamente modelos cuando se añade un nuevo proveedor

dataStorage.title: Almacenamiento de datos
dataStorage.description: Administrar datos de sesión locales del navegador. Los datos persistentes sobreviven a la recarga de la página y los reinicios del navegador.
dataStorage.persistChatDescription: Recordar mensajes, sugerencias y personas a través de sesiones
dataStorage.persistChatConfirm: Esto borrará todos los mensajes de chat guardados. ¿Continuar?
dataStorage.persistArenaDescription: Recordar estado del corchete y preguntar entre sesiones
dataStorage.persistArenaConfirm: Esto borrará todos los datos de arena guardados. ¿Continuar?
dataStorage.persistConversationDescription: Recordar estado de la conversación y ajustes entre sesiones
dataStorage.persistConversationConfirm: Esto borrará todos los datos de conversación guardados. ¿Continuar?
dataStorage.saveMatchHistoryDescription: Guardar automáticamente la arena completada y comparar sesiones
dataStorage.saveMatchHistoryDisabled: Historial de arena desactivado - entradas existentes conservadas
dataStorage.maxSavedMatches.description: Las partidas más antiguas se eliminan automáticamente cuando se alcanza el límite
dataStorage.historyLimitToast: Límite del historial establecido en {{count}} partidas
dataStorage.clearHistoryConfirm: ¿Eliminar todo el historial de arena? Esto no se puede deshacer.
dataStorage.clearHistoryAll.tooltip: Borrar toda la arena guardada y comparar el historial de sesiones
dataStorage.clearHistoryAllCleared: Se borró todo el historial de arena
dataStorage.clearCache.tooltip: Borrar todos los datos de cuota de proveedor en caché. Los datos nuevos se obtendrán en la próxima actualización
dataStorage.clearCacheConfirm: ¿Borrar todos los datos de cuota de proveedor en caché? Los datos nuevos se recuperarán en la próxima actualización.
dataStorage.dismissedErrorBannersDescription: Restablecer estados de error de la barra lateral descartados
dataStorage.reset.tooltip: Restablecer estados de error de la barra lateral descartados

backup.title: Copia de seguridad de base de datos
backup.description: Crea y descarga copias de seguridad de PostgreSQL. Utiliza pg_dump con formato personalizado para una compresión eficiente.
backup.restoreRequirements.masterKeyDescription: Las claves API del proveedor son cifradas AES-256-GCM. Restaurar con un MASTER_KEY diferente hará que todas las claves del proveedor sean irrecuperables.
backup.restoreRequirements.adminToken: El token de administrador no está en la copia de seguridad
backup.restoreRequirements.adminTokenDescriptio: Tu token de administración actual continuará funcionando después de la restauración.
backup.restoreRequirements.virtualKeysDescripti: Sólo se almacenan hash SHA-256. Si pierde las claves virtuales de texto plano, no se pueden recuperar de la copia de seguridad.
backup.selectBackupFile: Seleccionar copia de seguridad para restaurar
backup.noBackups: Aún no hay copias de seguridad.
backup.restoreSuccess: Base de datos restaurada. El servidor se está reiniciando…
backup.serverBackOnline: El servidor ha vuelto a estar en línea
backup.serverRestarting: El servidor está tardando más de lo esperado en reiniciar

rotation.title: Copia de seguridad periódica
rotation.enabledDescription: Crea automáticamente copias de seguridad a intervalos regulares y elimina las antiguas usando la rotación hijo/padre/abuelo.
rotation.intervalDescription: Con qué frecuencia crear una copia de seguridad automática (0 = deshabilitado).
rotation.sonRetentionDescription: Mantenga la copia de seguridad más reciente de cada uno de los últimos N días.
rotation.fatherRetentionDescription: Mantenga la copia de seguridad más reciente de cada una de las últimas N semanas.
rotation.grandfatherRetentionDescription: Mantenga la copia de seguridad más reciente de cada uno de los últimos N meses.
rotation.confirmEnableDescription: Habilitar copia de seguridad periódica limpiará las copias de seguridad existentes que quedan fuera del esquema de rotación de hijo/padre/abuelo.
rotation.confirmEnableWouldRemove: Esto eliminaría las siguientes {{count}} copia(s) de seguridad:
rotation.confirmEnableNoRemoval: No se eliminarían copias de seguridad existentes.
rotation.prunePreviewFailed: Error al previsualizar la limpieza de copia de seguridad
rotation.pruneFailed: Error al limpiar las copias de seguridad
rotation.pruneSuccess: Se han eliminado {{count}} copia(s) de seguridad

appearance.title: Apariencia

appearance.themeDescription: Cambiar entre los modos oscuro, claro y del sistema

toast.title: Notificaciones Toast
toast.description: Elegir dónde aparecen los brindis de notificación y cuánto tiempo permanecen visibles.
toast.testNotification: Notificación de prueba - verás tostadas aquí
toast.autoDismissDescription: Cuánto tiempo los brindis permanecen visibles antes de desaparecer
toast.fuseEffectDescription: Contorno animado de una mecha encendida que traza la notificación mientras cuenta atrás

dashboard.title: Actualizar Tablero
dashboard.description: Configurar la frecuencia con la que se actualizan automáticamente las estadísticas del panel y los gráficos. El botón de actualización manual se oculta cuando se establece en 10 segundos o más rápido.
dashboard.refreshInterval.description: A 10 segundos el botón de actualización manual está oculto. 0 para desactivar. Los cambios surtirán efecto en la próxima navegación al tablero.
dashboard.disabled: Panel de control auto-actualizar desactivado - usar actualización manual
dashboard.intervalSet_one: El panel de control se actualiza cada {{seconds}} segundo
dashboard.intervalSet_other: El panel de control se actualiza cada {{seconds}} segundos
dashboard.pleaseWaitBeforeRefreshing: Por favor espere antes de actualizar de nuevo

sidebarQuota.title: Citas de barra lateral
sidebarQuota.description: Configura la frecuencia con la que los datos del proveedor se actualizan en el panel de la barra lateral.
sidebarQuota.showQuotasPillDescription: Mostrar el panel de cuotas en la barra lateral
sidebarQuota.refreshInterval.description: Mínimo 1 minuto. 0 para desactivar. Los cambios tendrán efecto en la próxima actualización programada.
sidebarQuota.disabled: Barra lateral de cuota auto-refrescar desactivada - usar actualización manual
sidebarQuota.enabledQuotas: Cupos de barra lateral habilitados - píldora visible y refresco automático reanudada
sidebarQuota.disabledQuotas: Cupos laterales desactivados - píldora oculta y auto-refresca pausada

sidebarQuota.intervalSet_other: Actualización de la cuota establecida en cada {{minutes}} minutos

passkeys.title: Claves de acceso
passkeys.description: Registra una clave de acceso para iniciar sesión con tu huella digital, cara o clave de seguridad en lugar de escribir el token de administración.
passkeys.alreadyRegistered: Esta clave de acceso ya está registrada en este dispositivo

observability.json.description: Genera registros estructurados como un objeto JSON por línea, para recopiladores de registros de salida estándar como Fluent Bit, Vector o Promtail.
observability.json.instructions: Configure esta variable de entorno y reinicie el contenedor para generar registros en formato JSON:
observability.json.name: Registros JSON (stdout)
observability.metrics.description: Exponer las métricas de solicitud, token, latencia y disyuntor en /metrics para que Prometheus las recopile.
observability.metrics.instructions: Configure un token de rastreo específico para aprovisionar métricas de Prometheus. Ya se puede acceder a /metrics utilizando el token de administrador.
observability.metrics.name: Métricas de Prometheus
observability.otel.description: Envía los mismos registros estructurados a un colector de OpenTelemetry a través de OTLP (solo registros, sin rastreos ni métricas).
observability.otel.instructions: Dirige esto a tu punto final del colector OTLP y reinicia el contenedor para enviar los registros a través de OpenTelemetry:
observability.otel.name: Registros de OpenTelemetry (OTLP)
observability.title: Observabilidad y exportación de registros
observability.description: Model Hotel puede exportar sus registros y métricas estructurados a sistemas externos. Cada integración se habilita mediante una variable de entorno; los indicadores de estado que se muestran a continuación reflejan el estado actual. Nunca se exporta el contenido de las solicitudes ni de las respuestas, solo los metadatos de enrutamiento y medición.

alerts.apiUrl: URL de la API de Apprise
alerts.apiUrlDescription: URL base del contenedor apprise-api que ejecutas (por ejemplo, http://apprise:8000).
alerts.description: Envía notificaciones push de eventos destacados (una interrupción del servicio del proveedor, la activación de un cortacircuitos) a Telegram, correo electrónico, Discord y otras plataformas a través de un contenedor Apprise sin estado que tú mismo ejecutas. Nunca se envía el contenido de la solicitud, solo el resumen del evento.
alerts.enableDescription: Reenvía los eventos seleccionados a tu punto final de Apprise.

event.circuit_breaker_half_open: Se está comprobando el proveedor (disyuntor medio abierto)
event.discovery_provider_failed: El proveedor ha fallado durante la detección
event.failover_sync_error: Error en la sincronización del grupo de conmutación por error

events.title: Eventos sobre los que se debe notificar

snippets.description: Ejemplos de formatos de URL de Apprise para servicios populares: copia uno, rellena tus datos y pégalo más arriba.

alerts.targetDescription: Tu URL de Apprise: se almacena cifrada y no se muestra nunca más. Separa los distintos destinos con ;.
alerts.testFailed: Error en la notificación de prueba: {{message}}
alerts.testHint: Activa las alertas y configura tanto una URL de la API de Apprise como un destino para enviar una prueba.
alerts.testSent: Se ha enviado una notificación de prueba.
alerts.title: Alertas

anthropic.topP: top_p está obsoleto en los modelos Antrópicos actuales; usa top_k en su lugar
anthropic.frequencyPenalty: Antrópico utiliza un único parámetro de penalizaciones, no una frecuencia/presencia separada
anthropic.presencePenalty: Antrópico utiliza un único parámetro de penalizaciones, no una frecuencia/presencia separada
anthropic.minP: No soportado por la API Antrópica
anthropic.reasoningEffort: No soportado por la API Antrópica

google.frequencyPenalty: Gemini no soporta sanciones de frecuencia/presencia
google.presencePenalty: Gemini no soporta sanciones de frecuencia/presencia
google.topK: Gemini top_k se comporta de forma diferente a OpenAI top_k; use top_p en su lugar
google.minP: No soportado por la API de Google Gemini
google.reasoningEffort: No soportado por la API de Google Gemini

cohere.topK: Cohere utiliza un parámetro 'k' diferente; no recomendado
cohere.minP: No soportado por la API de Cohere
cohere.reasoningEffort: No soportado por la API de Cohere

openai.minP: No es parte de la API OpenAI
openai.topK: No es parte de la API OpenAI

deepseek.minP: No soportado por la API de DeepSeek
deepseek.topK: No soportado por la API de DeepSeek
deepseek.reasoningEffort: No soportado por la API de DeepSeek

ollama.minP: El soporte varía según el modelo subyacente; no está disponible universalmente

ollamaCloud.minP: El soporte varía según el modelo subyacente; no está disponible universalmente

zaiCoding.minP: No soportado por el código z.ai
